name: Round Top Loop — Sibley Volcanic Preserve
notes: Beautiful morning hike through the volcanic formations at Sibley. The quarry pit is fascinating — you can clearly see the layered basalt from ancient lava flows. Great views of the Bay from the upper ridge. Spotted lupine and California poppies on the south-facing slopes. The interpretive signs along the trail explain the 10-million-year-old volcanic history. A perfect short loop when you want geology and wildflowers without a huge time commitment.

name: Hand-Drawn Wildcat Gorge Plan
notes: Rough route sketched on the map — want to explore the Wildcat Gorge trail connecting Tilden down to the creek. Need to check seasonal access and whether the lower section is passable after rain.
